story.iWant: existing tests refactored to use the new interface mocks from testutils
story.soThat: all framework tests follow consistent patterns and demonstrate proper usage of the interface-driven architecture

task.name: Analyze existing test patterns and create migration plan

subtasks.subtask: Audit all tests in test/ directory to identify dependencies and mocking needs
Create mapping from existing tests to interface mock requirements
Design test structure supporting both unit and integration patterns
Plan migration sequence to minimize disruption

task.name: Refactor service tests to use interface mocks

subtasks.subtask: Migrate test/endor_hybrid_service_test.go to use MockEndorHybridService and MockEndorService
Update tests to demonstrate interface usage patterns for service instantiation
Add unit test variants using mocked dependencies with fast execution
Preserve integration test coverage using testutils infrastructure

task.name: Refactor resource and schema tests with interface patterns

subtasks.subtask: Migrate test/resource_test.go to use MockRepository and MockConfigProvider
Update test/schema_test.go to demonstrate interface-driven schema validation
Apply build tags for unit vs integration test separation
Ensure test performance improvement through interface mocking

task.name: Update swagger and API tests with mock implementations

subtasks.subtask: Refactor test/swagger_test.go to use interface mocks for API documentation testing
Update tests to support dependency injection patterns for Epic 2 preparation
Create examples showing how to test API generation with different service configurations
Validate that mocked services generate correct swagger documentation

task.name: Establish testing best practices and documentation

subtasks.subtask: Create comprehensive testing examples in sdk/ showing refactored patterns
Document testing strategies for interface-driven framework development
Add testing guidelines for Epic 2 (dependency injection) and Epic 3 (service composition)
Create migration guide for developers updating existing framework-based projects
Benchmark test execution time improvements from interface mocking
Verify test coverage maintained or improved across all test suites
Validate build tag separation enables fast unit test execution
Create performance comparison report showing benefits of interface-driven testing

acceptanceCriteria.criteria: All existing tests in test/ directory refactored to use interface mocks from sdk/testutils package
Tests demonstrate proper usage of EndorServiceInterface, EndorHybridServiceInterface, RepositoryInterface, and configuration interfaces
Refactored tests maintain current test coverage while improving execution speed through mocking
Tests organized with //go:build unit and //go:build integration tags following layered testing strategy
Refactored tests serve as examples for developers using the framework's interface-driven architecture
Tests structured to support Epic 2's dependency injection patterns with clear separation of concerns

doc.title: endor-sdk-go Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Framework must support unit testing without MongoDB dependencies through interface mocking, provide test utility package with pre-built mocks, enable testing service composition patterns in isolation

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Interface Foundation & Testability
doc.snippet: Refactor existing test files to demonstrate interface-based testing approach. Separate unit tests (mocked dependencies) from integration tests (real dependencies). Create examples of testing service composition patterns in isolation

doc.title: endor-sdk-go Refactored Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Following Go's interface philosophy of "accept interfaces, return structs". Interface strategy with RepositoryInterface for data access, ConfigProviderInterface for configuration, EndorServiceInterface and EndorHybridServiceInterface for services

doc.title: endor-sdk-go Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: Framework components become mockable and testable. FR5: Unit testing without MongoDB dependencies, FR6: Test utility package with pre-built mocks, FR7: Test service composition patterns in isolation

artifact.reason: Primary test file requiring refactoring to use interface mocks instead of concrete implementations
Resource testing patterns that need repository interface mocks
Schema tests that should demonstrate interface-driven validation patterns
API documentation tests that need service interface mocking
Available mock implementations for test refactoring
Test builders for creating configured mock services
Helper functions for test setup and interface validation
Core service interfaces that tests must demonstrate usage of
Repository interface for data access mocking in tests

constraints.constraint: Tests must maintain current coverage while improving execution speed through interface mocking
Build tag organization with //go:build unit for mocked tests and //go:build integration for database tests
All interface usage patterns must demonstrate proper dependency injection preparation for Epic 2
Test migration must preserve existing test logic while updating instantiation patterns
Fast unit test execution targeting under 100ms total runtime using interface mocks
Interface compliance validation using var _ Interface = (*Implementation)(nil) pattern
Integration tests continue using real implementations with testutils infrastructure

interface.name: EndorServiceInterface
interface.signature: type EndorServiceInterface interface { GetResource() string GetDescription() string GetMethods() map[string]EndorServiceAction GetPriority() *int GetVersion() string Validate() error }

interface.name: EndorHybridServiceInterface
interface.signature: type EndorHybridServiceInterface interface { GetResource() string GetResourceDescription() string GetPriority() *int WithCategories(categories []EndorHybridServiceCategory) EndorHybridServiceInterface WithActions(fn func(getSchema func() RootSchema) map[string]EndorServiceAction) EndorHybridServiceInterface ToEndorService(metadataSchema Schema) EndorServiceInterface Validate() error }

interface.name: RepositoryInterface
interface.signature: type RepositoryInterface[T any] interface { Create(ctx context.Context, entity T) (T, error) Read(ctx context.Context, id string) (T, error) Update(ctx context.Context, entity T) error Delete(ctx context.Context, id string) error List(ctx context.Context, filter interface{}) ([]T, error) }

interface.name: ConfigProviderInterface
interface.signature: type ConfigProviderInterface interface { GetServerPort() string GetDocumentDBUri() string IsHybridResourcesEnabled() bool IsDynamicResourcesEnabled() bool Validate() error }

tests.standards: Framework uses Go testing with testify/mock for behavior verification and testify/assert for assertions. Interface compliance testing follows var _ Interface = (*Implementation)(nil) pattern. Build tags separate //go:build unit tests (mocked dependencies, fast execution) from //go:build integration tests (real dependencies, database setup). Mock implementations use testify/mock with configurable behavior and expectation validation.
tests.locations: test/ directory for legacy tests requiring refactoring, sdk/ package structure for new interface-based tests, sdk/testutils/ for mock implementations and test builders

ideas.idea: Migrate test/endor_hybrid_service_test.go to use MockEndorHybridService demonstrating interface usage patterns
Convert test/resource_test.go to use MockRepository for fast data access testing without MongoDB
Update test/schema_test.go with build tags showing unit vs integration test separation
Refactor test/swagger_test.go to demonstrate service interface mocking for API generation testing
Create comprehensive testing examples showing dependency injection patterns for Epic 2 preparation
Benchmark test execution time improvements to validate under 100ms target for unit tests
